capabilities: Implement a structured evaluation process for source credibility in knowledge graphs, integrating criteria and metrics specific to EVM Solidity-derived data.

procedure.action: Run SPARQL queries to extract nodes with high centrality scores and verify their source against a trusted registry using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Invoke Solidity static analysis tools to identify contract vulnerabilities and flag associated data sources by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Execute PageRank algorithm to rank sources by credibility and cross-reference with external validation datasets by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.

Set up automated alerts for data inconsistencies using a rule-based engine with predefined credibility thresholds using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Conduct manual audits on a random sample of high-impact nodes to ensure compliance with credibility standards using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Establish a robust framework for evaluating source credibility in knowledge graphs derived from EVM Solidity, ensuring accurate and reliable knowledge base construction.
title: Source Credibility Evaluation in Knowledge Graphs

failureConditions: Inaccurate credibility scores lead to misinformation propagation; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

High false positive rate in source flagging reduces trust in the system; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Manual audits reveal systemic biases in automated credibility assessments; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
successConditions: Credibility scores align with external validation datasets within a 5% margin; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Automated alerts correctly identify 95% of data inconsistencies; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Manual audits confirm automated assessments in 90% of cases; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
